title: Telegram media (photo/video/document/voice) bypasses process_input and fails on Markdown parse — user sees no response
current_state: Ash reports: 'Telegram pi isnt reading or analysing anything other than text msges'. Diagnosis: (1) handle_photo/handle_video/handle_document call MediaTools.analyze_* directly and reply with raw vision text — image never enters process_input, never lands in L1/L2 memory, no consciousness prompt applied, no conversational continuity. (2) bot is constructed with parse_mode='Markdown' (line 45). Vision outputs from Gemini/Claude commonly contain unescaped *, _, [ chars; Telegram API rejects with 400 'can't parse entities' on reply_to(); handlers catch silently. (3) bubble.py dispatcher line 243 has comment '(media-only bubble — handler not yet wired)' confirming media routing is structurally incomplete. (4) silent_failures.db shows 14 telegram.bubble_dispatch RuntimeErrors today plus 10 thinking.both_providers_failed but ZERO telegram.photo/video/document entries — meaning either media handlers don't fire OR they fire silently and replies fail invisibly.
structural_problem: Pi is supposed to be a continuous agent — vision content should flow into memory and shape future turns. Current design treats media as one-shot Q&A against vision API. User correctly identifies this as 'not reading or analysing or getting back'. Markdown parse_mode compounds the problem by breaking the one-shot reply path too.
target_state: All four media handlers (photo/video/document/voice) route through Pi's process_input by constructing a structured user_text containing the vision/transcript output + caption. Pi's main loop processes it normally: writes to L1/L2/L3, applies consciousness prompt, generates contextual response. Reply sent via parse_mode=None to bypass Markdown breakage. Audio/voice keeps its existing transcribe+respond pattern but also fixes the parse_mode. Adds a single private helper _send_plain_chunks for plain-text chunked sends.
files_affected: testing/test_telegram_media_routing.py (NEW) — verify handlers call process_input with vision text
design_notes: Add private method _send_plain_chunks(bot, chat_id_or_message, text) that splits text into 4096-char chunks and sends each with parse_mode=None. Used by all media handlers.

Pattern for each media handler: (1) auth check, (2) typing indicator, (3) download file, (4) analyze (vision/STT), (5) build user_text wrapping the analysis with a brief frame ('Photo received with caption ...; vision analysis: ...; respond as Pi'), (6) call self._process_text(user_text) → Pi's normal pipeline, (7) _send_plain_chunks(reply), (8) cleanup temp file.
Voice handler already uses _process_text — keep that, but switch the wrapping reply ('You said: ... \n\n ...') to _send_plain_chunks. The TTS audio path is unaffected.
Error path: on analyze failure, do NOT abort — pass the error string as user_text so Pi can acknowledge ('I couldn't see that photo because ...'). User gets a real Pi response, not silent failure.
Keep bot constructor parse_mode='Markdown' unchanged — text path relies on it for italic/bold in Pi's responses. Only the media paths use plain.
Do NOT touch _dispatch_bubble — the 'media-only bubble' branch is a separate (deferred) feature. Filing as backlog elsewhere.
Auth + group-chat filtering: media handlers don't currently filter group chats (any photo dropped on Pi gets analyzed). Out of scope for this fix; leave as-is.
success_criteria: A photo with caption 'what is this?' arrives → handle_photo downloads → analyze_image returns vision text → process_input called with frame containing vision text → Pi's response arrives back as plain Telegram message
Same flow for video, document, voice/audio
Photo analysis appears in logs/turns.jsonl (because process_input is called)
Reply with text containing raw * / _ chars sends successfully (parse_mode=None confirms)
Vision API failure → Pi receives error string as input → Pi replies with a graceful acknowledgement
Existing test_telegram_bubble_integration.py + test_telegram_lifecycle.py + test_stt.py all PASS
risk_notes: Risk: routing media through process_input adds Claude call latency (~1-3s) AND token cost (~2-3k extra tokens per photo). Acceptable — current behavior is broken anyway. Risk: in group chats, media handler doesn't filter for @mention/reply-to-Pi. Existing pre-fix behavior; not regressing. Risk: TTS audio response in voice handler — keep working; only change the text-reply parse_mode.
migration_plan: 2. Refactor handle_photo: route through _process_text after analyze.
5. Refactor handle_voice: keep STT + _process_text, only swap the user-facing reply to plain.
6. Write test_telegram_media_routing.py — mock telebot + MediaTools, assert _process_text called with vision-text-containing input.
